title: lathyrus luteus subsp. aureus
intro: Lathyrus luteus subsp. aureus, also known as the golden peacock lathyrus, is a subspecies of the wild pea plant that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lathyrus luteus subsp. aureus include its ability to act as an antioxidant, reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies.
